items.title: DaterangeTV
items.text: DaterangeTV is a custom template variable for MODX Revolution. The template variable displays two interdependent datapicker inputs.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• MODX Revolution 2.6+
  • PHP 7.2+

items.title: Features
items.text:
• Two interdependent date picker inputs that allow the backend user to enter a date range.
  • Snippet/Output filter to display the date range with formatting options in the frontend.

items.title: License
items.text: The project is licensed under the GPLv2 license.

items.title: Translations
items.text: Translations of the package can be made for the Default Lexicon, the Properties Lexicon and the System Setting Lexicon

items.title: Changelog
items.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix retrieving JSON snippet properties

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix editing template variable properties in MODX 3.0.1

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix removing plugin events

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix OnDocFormRender was not found!

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Code refactoring
  • Full MODX 3 compatibility

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Allow deselecting a selected second template variable for the end part of the daterange

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Change log level of not existing template variable message to debug
  • PHP/MODX version check
  • Normalized/refactored code
  • Prevent an error, when $modx->resource is not set

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Bugfix for allowBlank option [#10]

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Bugfix for mandatory first date even if allowBlank is set to true [#10]

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Bugfix for an issue when allowBlank is set to false [#9]
  • Bugfix for years first check in DaterangeTV.Renderer

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Improved backwards compatibility when switching a DaterangeTV to use a second TV for an end value [#6]

items.title: Added
items.text: • Optional second template variable for the end part of the daterange. Could be set to i.e. a hidden template variable

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Allow date format ordering other than d-m-y (thanks to smg6511)
  • Added stripEqualParts snippet property
  • Log only errors with enabled debug snippet property

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Improved support for manager_date_format and daterangetv.manager_format
  • Improved handling of invalid values in DaterangeTV.Renderer

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Bugfix for snippet call in manager context (i.e. MIGX column renderer)

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Added column renderer (for MIGX/Collections etc.)
  • Added default output system settings

items.title: Added
items.text: • Added snippet/output filter

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Removed debug code

items.title: Added
items.text: • Initial release for MODX Revolution

items.title: Contributors
items.text:
The DaterangeTV project was started in 2013 by Thomas Jakobi.

Many thanks to all who contributed, whether by creating pull requests, submitting bug reports, or donating.

items.title: Install from MODX Extras
items.text: Search for DaterangeTV in the Package Manager of your MODX installation and install it in there.

items.title: Manual installation
items.text:
If you can't access the MODX Extras Repository in your MODX installation, you can manually install DaterangeTV.

  • Download the transport package from MODX Extras (or one of the pre built transport packages in _packages)
  • Upload the zip file to your MODX installation's core/packages folder or upload it manually in the MODX Package Manager.
  • In the MODX Manager, navigate to the Package Manager page, and select 'Search locally for packages' from the dropdown button.
  • DaterangeTV should now show up in the list of available packages. Click the corresponding 'Install' button and follow the instructions to complete the installation.

items.title: Build it from source
items.text: To build and install the package from source you could use Git Package Management. The GitHub repository of DaterangeTV contains a config.json to build that package locally. Use this option, if you want to debug DaterangeTV and/or contribute bugfixes and enhancements.

items.title: Input Options
items.text:
The following input options could be set in template variable settings:

Setting Description Default Allow Blank If set to No, MODX will not allow the user to save the Resource until a valid, non-blank value has been entered in the From Date input. Yes Date Format The format must be valid according to Ext JS Date. Manager date format End Value Template Variable Template Variable that contains the end value of the daterange. If used, the DaterangeTV contains only the start value. The end value template variable should be created as a hidden template variable type. -

items.title: Output Options
items.text:
The following output options could be set in template variable settings if the output type is set to Date Range (From <> To). Another possibility is to assign the settings as properties to the MODX tag[^1].

[^1]: The daterange template tariable could be formatted in template or template chunks with the following tag syntax: [[*tvname? &format=`%e| %B |%Y` &locale=`de_DE.utf8`]]

Setting Description Default Date Format A between day, month and year by | separated list of strftime placeholders[^2]. %e| %B |%Y Separator String between the first and second part of the daterange. – Locale Locale the daterange strings are formatted with. MODX locale system setting

[^2]: If the output type is set to Date Range (From <> To) the output will be formatted removing equal days and months (and years - by showing only the start date).

items.title: Snippet/Output filter
items.text:
If the output options for the custom template variable could not be set (i.e. inside of MIGX) or if you want to use snippet or an output filter could be used. The snippet has the following properties:

Property Description Default tvname Name of the DaterangeTV. - docid Resource where the DaterangeTV value is received from. Current resource value Use your own value for the snippet output. The properties tvname and docid are ignored. - format A between day, month and year by | separated list of strftime placeholders[^2]. %e.| %B |%Y separator String between the first and second part of the daterange.  –  locale Locale the daterange strings are formatted with. MODX locale system setting stripEqualParts Strip equal parts from the date range output (i.e. strip the year, if start and end of the date range have the same year). Yes

So the following snippet call could be used: 

[[Daterange? 
&value=`2013-01-01||2013-01-02` 
&format=` %d.| %b. |%Y` 
&separator=` – ` 
&locale=`de_DE.utf8`
]]

The snippet could work as an output filter, but then the options have to be a json encoded array:

[[*daterangetv:daterange=`{"format":"%d|%m.|%Y","separator":"–","locale":"de_DE.utf8"}`]]

items.title: Column Renderer
items.text:
The package contains a column renderer for i.e. MIGX or Collections. In Collections you have to insert DaterangeTV.Renderer in the renderer option of the grid column. The name option has to be filled with the template variable name prepended with tv_.

You can't render the full value, when the end value template variable is enabled. Then the colum will only contain the first value and both values could be rendered with the DaterangeTV.Renderer. The format of the date can be changed with the system setting daterangetv.manager_format and the separator between two dates with the system setting daterangetv.separator. If you want to use only one column, you have to fill the Collections snippet renderer option with Daterange.

items.title: System Settings
items.text:
DaterangeTV uses the following system settings in the namespace daterangetv:

Key Name Description Default daterangetv.debug Debug Log debug information in the MODX error log. No daterangetv.format Date Format A between day, month and year by | separated list with strftime formats. "%e. | %B | %Y" daterangetv.manager_format Manager Date Format A between day, month and year by | separated list with Ext JS Date formats to format the Collections/MIGX value. "j. | F | Y" daterangetv.separator Separator String between the first and second part of the daterange to format the Collections/MIGX value. " – "
